items.title: Petrol prices leap to national average of 198 cents per litre, new data shows
items.summary: New data has confirmed a significant rise in fuel costs, with the national average petrol price increasing 17 cents over the week to Sunday, to 198 cents a litre, as analysts warn there's more room for it to rise.

items.title: Electoral review proposes scrapping two Queensland seats, changing boundaries of dozens more
items.summary: The first review into Queensland's electoral boundaries since 2017 has recommended for two electorates to be abolished, two more created, and almost 50 changed.

items.title: How Donald Trump's latest Iran war update fits into the US administration's shifting statements
items.summary: US President Donald Trump has said the war in Iran could be over "very soon", but has still not offered a definitive end date for the conflict.

items.title: Public health patient demands apology after private health info given to media
items.summary: Monica Wohlstadt is the second person in a matter of days to raise concerns about how the SA health minister's office used personal patient information to "background" journalists ahead of the publication of stories.

items.title: Lachlan Bowles was wearing Nazi armband during Kellerberrin shooting, inquest hears
items.summary: A man who fatally shot his colleague at a Wheatbelt grain silo before turning the weapon on himself had undiagnosed mental health issues and was exploring extremist ideology, an inquest hears.

items.title: Migration agent shares details of secret operation to get Iranian football asylum seekers alone

items.title: The government is not speaking plainly about joining the war effort in the Middle East
items.summary: It's a reasonable question for Australians to ask given the government is sending a sophisticated reconnaissance plane — crewed by dozens of military personnel — to the Middle East. And legal experts say the answer is yes.

items.title: How a war in the Middle East caused Australia's sudden fuel price hikes
items.summary: If you've topped up on fuel this week, you likely would have noticed prices at the bowser have jumped a fair bit. Here's a quick breakdown on why that happened.

items.title: Emotional David Littleproud steps down as leader of the National Party
items.summary: The Queenslander cried as he spoke to reporters, saying it had been a "pretty rough" time since the May election.

items.title: Petrol prices surge across Asia, prompting rationing and price caps
items.summary: From closing schools to daily limits, the surging cost of oil prompts countries across Asia to take action to ensure fuel supply and keep prices in check.

items.title: Zac Lomax admits he is a rugby union 'novice', but has no regrets about end to NRL career
items.summary: Rugby recruit Zac Lomax knows he has a long way to go to fulfil his dream of becoming a dual international and playing for the Wallabies at the 2027 World Cup.

items.title: Kyle Sandilands insists he is not in breach of $100m contract over on-air fight with co-host Jackie O
items.summary: In his first statement since he was ordered off the air after a blow-up with co-host Jackie "O" Henderson, Sandilands calls on ARN Media, owner of KIIS FM, to "honour our agreement" and let him return to the Kyle and Jackie O Show.

items.title: New laws to temporarily ban people coming to Australia from designated countries
items.summary: Visitors from some countries could be temporarily blocked from Australia by a federal government concerned about a wave of people overstaying their visas due to the war in the Middle East.

items.title: Darwin issued a boil water alert, here's what you need to know
items.summary: A boil water alert has been issued for Darwin, in addition to Katherine, Beswick and Tindal. The alert comes after issues with the pump station at Darwin's main dam. Here's what you need to know about your water.

items.title: Indigenous artworks taken on 5,000km road trip from the Pilbara to Biennale of Sydney
items.summary: From the Pilbara to Penrith, an Indigenous WA community has taken its prized cultural and artistic possessions across the country for the Biennale of Sydney.

items.title: Five things I do (as a therapist) when anxiety takes over
items.summary: No-one is harder on me than me. Anxiety thrives on this. Along the way, I've built a toolkit that helps me regulate, reconnect and come back to myself.

items.title: Middle East conflict hiking fertiliser costs for Australian farmers but what does it mean for grocery prices?
items.summary: The Middle East conflict is driving up the cost of an essential fertiliser for Australian farmers, but there are other factors more likely to affect grocery prices.

items.summary: As Alex Johnston moves to the edge of forever in his chase of the all-time try-scoring record, rugby league must brace itself for a chance that only comes around once or twice a century.

items.title: Iranian soccer players escape minders and secure asylum in Australia

The government has granted asylum to five athletes who escaped from their hotel with assistance from Australian Federal Police.

items.title: Hungary seizes money and gold in deepening diplomatic spat with Ukraine
items.summary: Hungary has seized around $115 million and gold from a Ukrainian bank transfer and temporarily detained seven Ukrainians, as Budapest pressures Kyiv to restart oil shipments from Russia.

items.title: Labor to pass super tax changes with support of Greens
items.summary: The legislation comes as speculation builds that the federal government will pursue more ambitious tax changes in the May budget.

items.summary: Never mind artificial intelligence, this is dragon intelligence. A new play commissioned for the Perth Festival explores the importance of human creativity, even if AI can help with an unfinished novel.

items.title: Margaret River to survey homeless population as housing crisis bites holiday hotspot

items.summary: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese confirms the Australian Defence Force will deploy an E-7A Wedgetail aircraft to the Gulf to help protect and defend the region, providing long-range reconnaissance activity.

items.summary: The Australian market closed 1 per cent higher, regaining some of yesterday's losses. Catch up on the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Queensland calls on Energy Minister Chris Bowen for update on government's fuel security plan
items.summary: Queensland Energy Minister David Janetzki has written to federal counterpart Chris Bowen warning of major impacts on daily life.

items.summary: David Littleproud announces he is stepping down as Nationals leader, saying he believes it is time to transition to a new leader.

items.title: Dubai's influencers have a new rule: Don't mention the war
items.summary: As missiles and drones began lighting up the sky over Dubai, many of the city's social media personalities started posting about it. Then the government stepped in.

items.title: Sharing of wrong patient's email provides rare public glimpse into the cunning world of political spin
items.summary: It is common for media advisors to provide reporters with background information in an effort to shape the way a story is reported, but the practice backfired spectacularly when the SA government shared an email from the wrong patient.

items.title: Sixth case of flesh-eating Buruli ulcer appears on NSW South Coast
items.summary: The sixth case of a flesh-eating Buruli ulcer has been detected on the NSW South Coast.

items.summary: The Australian Medical Association says politicians should not be using non-consensual patient information to make a point about the health system.

items.title: Scientists call for urgent funding as hundreds of CSIRO job cuts loom
items.summary: Scientists are calling on the Australian government to push back on a plan to scrap hundreds of jobs at the nation's science agency, as they await the release of further details this week.

items.title: Decline in GP home visits drives vulnerable people into care homes
items.summary: The number of GP home visits has declined by 85 per cent in Australia over the past three decades, which doctors say is driving vulnerable people into residential care and hospital prematurely.

items.title: Current and former Liberals are quietly behind two 'grassroots' childcare campaigns
items.summary: The childcare groups say they are the voices for everyday parents. What they do not mention is that they are supported by a network of current and former Liberals.

items.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Matildas learn quarterfinal opponent as North Korea sensationally protests offside call
items.summary: The Matildas will face North Korea in the quarterfinals of the Women's Asian Cup, after the North Koreans go down 2-1 to China in controversial circumstances at a soaking Western Sydney Stadium.

items.title: How the 'tough guy' of South-East Asia is reshaping Australia's largest neighbour
items.summary: Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto's big ambitions on the world stage and at home have alarmed his critics.

items.title: Brisbane Lions to fight Harris Andrews and Darcy Gardiner bans at AFL tribunal
items.summary: Brisbane begins its season with two pivotal cases at the AFL tribunal in a bid to not have its defensive stocks completely depleted heading into the first month.

items.title: Australian Border Force intercepts 150,000 units of melatonin amid crackdown on unregulated products
items.summary: Australian Border Force officials have intercepted more than 150,000 units of melatonin in four months, after authorities warned they would crack down on unregulated products.

items.title: ABC Shakespeare quiz: How well doest thou know his characters and quotes? Testeth thineself hither

items.title: Farmers, community groups call for wild deer to be labelled a pest as population balloons
items.summary: The Tasmanian government asked the public what it thought about how wild fallow deer were being managed. It received hundreds of submissions calling for deer to be labelled a pest and for improved zoning and hunting permits.

items.title: Five Iranian football players granted asylum amid fears of persecution

items.summary: Josh Burns also acknowledged the government had not always acted fast enough to deal with antisemitism and said it was necessary to be "humble" in the wake of the Bondi terror attack.

items.title: Emergency services prioritised as some regional petrol stations ration fuel
items.summary: Petrol stations in some WA towns begin restricting petrol sales to emergency services only, as impacts from the Iran war hit the state.

items.title: Big Red Bash site will not be dry enough to hold festival in July, search for new venue
items.summary: The event is still four months away, but organisers say the site will not be sufficiently dry in time.

items.title: As the Iran war rages on, historic oil price surge sends share markets into a spin
items.summary: For more than a year, traders have been pushing markets higher on the hopes of an AI-powered economic miracle and the notion of TACO — Trump Always Chickens Out. Now it looks like the penny has dropped.

items.title: Farmers stockpile diesel as Middle East conflict prompts fuel supply fears
items.summary: Farmers say consumers could soon feel the impact of fuel shortages and soaring prices at the checkout, as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupts global supply chains.

items.title: Saltwater crocodile found in dam hundreds of kilometres from normal habitat
items.summary: Authorities say they will rehome a 2.3 metre saltwater crocodile that was found in a dam near Gatton, hundreds of kilometres from its usual habitat.

items.title: Defying Donald Trump and stepping out of the shadows, Mojtaba Khamenei ascends
items.summary: US President Donald Trump said the worst-case scenario in Iran would be if Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's successor were "as bad as the previous person". In many ways, the appointment of his son, Mojtaba Khamenei, delivers him that very outcome.

items.summary: Former Essendon captain Zach Merrett is the centre of attention in the build-up to a round-one blockbuster against the club he attempted to join in the off-season.

items.title: Prince Harry and Meghan Markle confirm Australian visit in April
items.summary: The visit will mark seven years since they last touched down in the country for their successful tour as the newlywed Duke and Duchess of Sussex.
